key_insight: The paper shows that RLVR instability can be predicted from token-level gradient dynamics, where both the advantage sign and the current token distribution determine whether updates sharpen or flatten the policy.
engineering_relevance: This yields a simpler and more stable policy-optimization rule for reasoning models, helping practitioners reduce collapse during training while preserving or improving benchmark performance.
method: Derive a token-level gradient taxonomy for RLVR updates and their effects on next-token probabilities and entropy.
Introduce Winner Advantage Policy Optimization (WAPO), a clipped online policy-gradient objective that trains only on positive-advantage completions.
Evaluate WAPO against GRPO-style baselines across multiple model families on mathematical reasoning and multi-hop QA tasks.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports that WAPO improves training stability and matches or outperforms baselines across mathematical reasoning and multi-hop QA benchmarks.
watch_item: Because WAPO updates only on positive-advantage completions, implementation may depend strongly on reward quality and could reduce learning signal if positives are too sparse.
paper_title: A Gradient Perspective on RLVR Stability and Winner Advantage Policy Optimization
